Company Lennar in the community Our responsibility to the communities we live and conduct business in extends far beyond building and delivering quality homes. We are committed to giving back, doing the right thing for the right reason, and incorporating environmentally sustainable products and processes. The Lennar Foundation Created in 1989, our Lennar Foundation reflects our commitment to our Community. We partner with organizations that assist the less fortunate and improve their quality of life. Our foundation receives a contribution of $1,000 per home towards our Focused Acts of Caring and other grants, to support at-risk children, medical research, and more. Focused Acts of Caring Each December, our Divisions replace the usual corporate holiday parties with a Focused Acts of Caring kickoff for the coming year. Their efforts go beyond financial support to dedicating time and energy to help these non-profits achieve their goals. Dolphins Challenge Cancer Lennar proudly supports the Miami Dolphins Challenge Cancer (DCC). The DCC hosts 5k runs, cycling races and other events, and 100% of participant-raised funds go towards cancer research at the Sylvester Comprehensive Cancer Center. We encourage our Associates to get involved as volunteers or participants. pages.text:
Frequently asked questions Community Community What is a Community Development District (CDD) ? The CDD refers to a community that assesses fees to pay for infrastructure and amenities within the community. Are there currently any promotions or incentives for this community? Contact a consultant for this community to inquire about any current promotions or incentives. Where can I see the floorplan of a home? Select a Plan from a Community page and scroll down to find the selection "View floorplan." How do I find out the grand opening date for this community? To receive information about this community's grand opening, contact a Consultant to have your name added to the interest list. How do I find out if a homesite is available? Please visit the community page for your community of interest to see a list of all available homesites. How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? Visit our Contact Us page and find the "I am looking for a home" label. Click Contact a consultant, select the location you are interested in and select fromthe live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about Where can I find a list of available homesites? Visit the community page for the community of your interest to see a list of all available homesites. Is this community pet friendly? To inquire about pet regulations in this community contact a Consultant. You can also ask about pet policies while visiting one of our Welcome Home Centers and speaking with a consultant, or you can call the Welcome Home Center. How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? Visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. Financing Who do I contact for a closing date on our home? Please contact your Consultant to inquire about the closing date on your new Lennar home. How can I obtain a copy of my appraisal? You should have received a copy of your appraisal prior to closing. Should it be lost, you can call either your Loan Officer or Loan Processor to have a new copy sent to you. How do I learn more about financing a home? Visit our Products and services page and click on the "Apply now" link to speak with our knowledgeable loan officers to learn more about financing your new home. Does Lennar offer financing options? Yes, Lennar Mortgage, LLC is our full-service mortgage lender and financial services subsidiary. Whether you are buying your first or fifth home, refinancing a current mortgage, or funding a home repair project, Lennar Mortgage provides the most appropriate loan options to suit your needs. To learn more, visit our Products and services page and then click the "Apply now" link. Who do I call regarding my loan status? Please reach out to your Loan Officer to inquire about your loan status. Once your loan has gone to an underwriter, the Loan Officer will notify you of this action and give you the name of the Loan Processor who will handle your file after the underwriter. The Loan Processor will contact you via a phone call to discuss your credit approval and next steps for closing on your new home. If the loan has already closed, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service. You can contact them by visiting https://www.lennarmortgage.com/Contact-Us . Where can I find my loan coupons? You should have received three loan coupons for the first three payments when you signed your closing documents. You should also receive monthly coupons in the mail the month payment is due.* If you require assistance, please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262. *Please note: The payment coupons are informational only and have no monetary value. A check or money order must be enclosed with the coupon to be processed. Who do I call if I want to lock-in my interest rate? Please contact your Loan Officer to handle the lock-in of an interest rate on your mortgage. Who do I make my mortgage payment out to when writing a check? You will make your payment out to "Lennar Mortgage, LLC" until your loan is sold. Once your loan is sold, you will receive a notification of its sale and contact information for the new servicer, to whom you will make out all future payments. Who do I contact to make a payment by telephone? Please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262 any time between 9am and 5pm (EST) Monday through Friday. Can I make my mortgage payments online? Please send your mortgage payments to Lennar Mortgage’s Loan Servicing Department at the following address:
Lennar Mortgage LLC Attn: Customer Service 140 Fountain Pkwy. N., Suite 250 Saint Petersburg, FL 33716 If you have any questions, call us toll free at 1-800-741-8262.
Please note: Once your loan is sold, you will receive a notification of its sale and contact information for the new servicer, to whom you will make all future payments. When is my first mortgage payment due? Your first mortgage payment is typically due 30 to 45 days after closing. To verify, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service at 1-800-741-8262 or via email at [email protected] . When are my mortgage payments due (each month)? Your mortgage payment is due on the 1st of the month, and after the 15th there is a late charge. If you fail to make your mortgage payment within 30 days, a late payment will be reported to the credit agencies. You should have received payment coupons at your closing, but you will also receive a payment notification in the mail shortly before payment is due. If you have any questions, please email [email protected] , or pay by phone by calling the toll-free number 1-877-648-7462. Who can I refer my friends and relatives to if they interested in applying for a mortgage? If you know someone who is interested in applying for a mortgage, please direct them to call your Loan Officer. Who do I contact to refinance my home? Please contact your Loan Officer to inquire about refinancing, or visit our Products and services page and click on the "Apply now" link  to find a Loan Officer near you. Who do I send my documentation to? The fastest, easiest way to send your documentation to Lennar Mortgage is through their Digital Mortgage app. The app is free and keeps private information secure with bank-level encryption. You also have the option to mail, fax, or use our secure web portal to send in your documents, but the app will provide you ease and flexibility to close your loan faster. Homebuying Where can I determine the actual cost of a home? Please contact a Consultant for your community of interest to get the accurate cost of your home of interest. Where do I find the base price for a home? Base prices for new Lennar homes are available on our community pages. Contact a Consultant on the page for your community of interest to inquire about home prices. Can I build a Lennar home on my own property? Lennar does not offer build-to-contract homes on personal property. Should your needs change, or you consider purchasing a new Lennar home in one of our communities, you can visit our Contact us page. Once there, select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. Do I need a broker/agent to purchase a new home? No, you do not need a broker or agent to purchase a new home from Lennar or one of our subsidiaries. While consultants represent Lennar and not customers, they are knowledgeable experts who are with you every step of your new home journey, answering questions and ensuring your smooth transition into homeownership. Of course, you are welcome to work with an independent broker or agent if you choose. Not all communities offer a broker co-op commission. If I have a broker/agent, do they need to visit the community with me? An agent or broker is required to accompany and register with you on your first visit to the Welcome Home Center at your community of interest in order to qualify for a broker co-op commission. Select communities and areas may not offer a broker co-op commission, regardless of timing and registration, but you are always welcome to be accompanied by a broker or agent of your choice. Does Lennar have any future communities planned for my area? Lennar's building operations expand to include new markets every year. To see the most up-to-date list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page. What are the Homeowners Association (HOA) fees for my community of interest? You can find an approximate HOA fee on the community page on the website or you can contact a Consultant for your community of interest to learn about the HOA fees. What kind of home tours does Lennar offer? Lennar offers three ways to tour, a Virtual tour, a Self-Guided tour, and a Lennar Guided tour. The virtual tour is a combination of high-quality, 3D rendering, or a panoramic view of a model home. The self-guided tour is an onsite tour of a model home at your own pace. The Lennar Guided tour is an in-person one-on-one tour of a model home with a Consultant. Does Lennar offer an incentives for first time homebuyers or current Lennar homeowners? Promotions and incentives vary by community. Contact a home consultant for your community of interest to inquire about current incentives.
Screen reader support enabled. What is the meaning of homesite premium? Lennar offers premium options for those who want a larger home or one that is situated in a more desirable area of the community. For example, if you wish to live in a Lennar community with a golf course and own a home on the course, you might pay more for that desirable homesite. The same applies for the purchase of a larger homesite (property). Contact a Consultant for your community of interest to inquire about homesite premiums. What kind of innovative features does Lennar include in its homes? As part of Everything's Included® our homes include innovative features that help simplify your everyday life. To learn about our home automation products, please visit the Smart home technology section of our Everything's® Included page . What is a Next Gen® home? Lennar's Next Gen - The Home Within a Home® offers innovative floorplans with a separate Next Gen® suite that functions as its own home, to accommodate your changing lifestyle without sacrificing comfort or cost savings, whether you need space for wellness, work, learning or family. To learn more about Lennar's Next Gen® homes, please visit our Next Gen page . What is the Opendoor and Trade up program about? <span> Lennar has partnered with Opendoor to create the New Home Trade-up Program. Sell your eligible home to Opendoor for a cash offer at a time that coordinates with the closing date of your new Lennar home to avoid double-moves, double-mortgages, and storage units. For more information, please visit our Lennar and Opendoor program page . How do I find the price for a new home? New home prices vary between Lennar and our subsidiary members (i.e., Village Builders, CalAtlantic, and WCI) and communities. Prices fluctuate based on design upgrades, homesite premiums, special incentives, and additional factors. To inquire about home prices in your community of interest,visit that community's page and contact a Consultant. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for your area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. Where can I register a client? If your client has a community of interest, contact the consultant for that community to register your client and verify that community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Do you have a rental properties available? Lennar has rental properties available with our subsidiary member, Quarterra, in some of the nation’s most popular cities. To learn more, visit the Quarterra website . How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? On this page, click Ask a question to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. How do I determine the taxes for a new home? You can contact a Consultant to determine the taxes for a new home however you should also consult your personal tax advisor. Are there Lennar vacation homes? We have a variety of dream homes in the most desired vacation cities. If you know what area you're interested in vacationing in, use the search tool at the top right corner of this page to begin your home search. If you need help, visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. What is an Active Adult Community? These Lennar communities offer residents ages 55 and better the ultimate in carefree, innovative living. Enjoy resort-style amenities and activities, as well as the chance to explore and make lifelong friends in some of the nation’s most popular cities to retire. Pursuant to the Fair Housing Act, these communities are subject to certain restrictions on the ages of occupants, and some buyers may not be eligible. To learn more, visit our Active Adult Communities page . Where does Lennar build new homes? Lennar currently builds in multiple states and popular real estate markets across the nation. To view a full list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page. Homeowner What should I do if no one has contacted me about my existing warranty request? Please call your local Customer Care Team to inquire about your existing request and have the warranty request number available if you were sent one via email.
If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from live chat, message us, or call us to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Who do I contact about obtaining my mortgage interest statement for income tax purposes? Please contact your mortgage lender to inquire about your mortgage interest statement and how you can obtain a copy for income tax purposes. What is the phone number for my community's HOA? You can reach out to your local Customer Care Team to help you retrieve your HOA's phone number. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I find information regarding my previously owned/current Lennar home (paint colors, cabinet manufacturer, etc.)? To receive more information on your home, please contact your local Customer Care Team. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Who do I contact if I have questions or concerns about a warranty? To inquire about a warranty, please contact the local Customer Care team for your area. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. What are the Home Owner's Association (HOA) fees for my community? You can reach out to your local Customer Care Team to help you retrieve this information. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I get the floorplan of my home? To retrieve the floorplan for your home, sign into your myLennar account and select "My home details" from the navigation bar. Scroll down to find your floorplan.* If your floorplan is unavailable, please contact your local Customer Care Team for assistance; you can email them from your myLennar dashboard by clicking on the Contact Customer Care link and submitting the form. *Please note: The floorplan that is available is the marketing floorplan for your homesite and not the detailed blueprint of construction drawings. Detailed blueprints of construction are a matter of company privacy. Options and Upgrades Can I make changes to Lennar floorplans? Available changes to Lennar floorplans vary by community. Find your community of interest and contact a Consultant to learn more. What kind of interior design and add-on options are available for my home? To learn more about interior design and add-on options, contact a consultant for your community of interest. Can I customize my home or get additional upgrades? To learn more about customizations and additional upgrades, contact a consultant for your community of interest. Does Lennar have any green or energy saving initiatives or technologies? Yes, Lennar homes are built with energy-efficient construction and features which vary by community but can include solar panels, LED lighting, and ENERGY STAR® certified appliances. To learn more about available energy-saving features, visit our Everything’s Included® page and click on the Energy-conscious features link or you can view the list of “Included Features” on a Community page. Other How can I apply for a career with Lennar? You can search and apply for available career opportunities with Lennar by visiting our Careers page . Please note that for each application, you will need to submit a copy of your resume. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for you area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. If I am a broker/agent, where do I find information for my client? If you know what community your client is interested in, visit that community's page and select ‘Contact a consultant’ to register your client and verify the community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Otherwise, select the drop down at the top of this page, click 'Contact us' and select 'Business inquiries.' Complete the Business inquiry form to have a consultant contact you regarding your client’s new home needs. How do I update my contact preferences? Sign in to your myLennar account to update your contact preferences. Once you sign in, click My account and then click Contacts to update your communication preferences. How can I suggest improvements for this site? To report errors with our website, select the drop down at the top of this page, click Contact us and select Feedback. Complete the Feedback form to tell us how we're doing or to share your thoughts.
When completing the form, be sure to select "Website errors" under Select a topic. If you want to suggest improvements, select "Comments " from the topic options . Lennar builds new homes in 0 states across the country View all regions Contact us Home buying questions Warranty / service request Business inquiries Employment verification Feedback Investor Relations Legal Legal & disclaimers Accessibility policy Privacy policy Terms & conditions Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information - Comm Preferences Cookie Preferences Privacy Request Careers Investor Marketplace Blog FAQs Copyright © 2026 Lennar Corporation. Whether you are buying your first or fifth home, refinancing a current mortgage, or funding a home repair project, Lennar Mortgage provides the most appropriate loan options to suit your needs. To learn more, visit our Products and services page and then click the "Apply now" link. Who do I call regarding my loan status? Please reach out to your Loan Officer to inquire about your loan status. Once your loan has gone to an underwriter, the Loan Officer will notify you of this action and give you the name of the Loan Processor who will handle your file after the underwriter. The Loan Processor will contact you via a phone call to discuss your credit approval and next steps for closing on your new home. If the loan has already closed, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service. You can contact them by visiting https://www.lennarmortgage.com/Contact-Us . Where can I find my loan coupons? You should have received three loan coupons for the first three payments when you signed your closing documents. You should also receive monthly coupons in the mail the month payment is due.* If you require assistance, please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262. *Please note: The payment coupons are informational only and have no monetary value. A check or money order must be enclosed with the coupon to be processed. Who do I call if I want to lock-in my interest rate? Please contact your Loan Officer to handle the lock-in of an interest rate on your mortgage. Who do I make my mortgage payment out to when writing a check? You will make your payment out to "Lennar Mortgage, LLC" until your loan is sold. Once your loan is sold, you will receive a notification of its sale and contact information for the new servicer, to whom you will make out all future payments. Who do I contact to make a payment by telephone? Please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262 any time between 9am and 5pm (EST) Monday through Friday. Can I make my mortgage payments online? Please send your mortgage payments to Lennar Mortgage’s Loan Servicing Department at the following address:

When is my first mortgage payment due? Your first mortgage payment is typically due 30 to 45 days after closing. To verify, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service at 1-800-741-8262 or via email at [email protected] .
When is my first mortgage payment due?

[email protected]
When are my mortgage payments due (each month)? Your mortgage payment is due on the 1st of the month, and after the 15th there is a late charge. If you fail to make your mortgage payment within 30 days, a late payment will be reported to the credit agencies. You should have received payment coupons at your closing, but you will also receive a payment notification in the mail shortly before payment is due. If you have any questions, please email [email protected] , or pay by phone by calling the toll-free number 1-877-648-7462.

Who do I send my documentation to? The fastest, easiest way to send your documentation to Lennar Mortgage is through their Digital Mortgage app. The app is free and keeps private information secure with bank-level encryption. You also have the option to mail, fax, or use our secure web portal to send in your documents, but the app will provide you ease and flexibility to close your loan faster.

Homebuying Where can I determine the actual cost of a home? Please contact a Consultant for your community of interest to get the accurate cost of your home of interest. Where do I find the base price for a home? Base prices for new Lennar homes are available on our community pages. Contact a Consultant on the page for your community of interest to inquire about home prices. Can I build a Lennar home on my own property? Lennar does not offer build-to-contract homes on personal property. Should your needs change, or you consider purchasing a new Lennar home in one of our communities, you can visit our Contact us page. Once there, select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. Do I need a broker/agent to purchase a new home? No, you do not need a broker or agent to purchase a new home from Lennar or one of our subsidiaries. While consultants represent Lennar and not customers, they are knowledgeable experts who are with you every step of your new home journey, answering questions and ensuring your smooth transition into homeownership. Of course, you are welcome to work with an independent broker or agent if you choose. Not all communities offer a broker co-op commission. If I have a broker/agent, do they need to visit the community with me? An agent or broker is required to accompany and register with you on your first visit to the Welcome Home Center at your community of interest in order to qualify for a broker co-op commission. Select communities and areas may not offer a broker co-op commission, regardless of timing and registration, but you are always welcome to be accompanied by a broker or agent of your choice. Does Lennar have any future communities planned for my area? Lennar's building operations expand to include new markets every year. To see the most up-to-date list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page. What are the Homeowners Association (HOA) fees for my community of interest? You can find an approximate HOA fee on the community page on the website or you can contact a Consultant for your community of interest to learn about the HOA fees. What kind of home tours does Lennar offer? Lennar offers three ways to tour, a Virtual tour, a Self-Guided tour, and a Lennar Guided tour. The virtual tour is a combination of high-quality, 3D rendering, or a panoramic view of a model home. The self-guided tour is an onsite tour of a model home at your own pace. The Lennar Guided tour is an in-person one-on-one tour of a model home with a Consultant. Does Lennar offer an incentives for first time homebuyers or current Lennar homeowners? Promotions and incentives vary by community. Contact a home consultant for your community of interest to inquire about current incentives.
Screen reader support enabled. What is the meaning of homesite premium? Lennar offers premium options for those who want a larger home or one that is situated in a more desirable area of the community. For example, if you wish to live in a Lennar community with a golf course and own a home on the course, you might pay more for that desirable homesite. The same applies for the purchase of a larger homesite (property). Contact a Consultant for your community of interest to inquire about homesite premiums. What kind of innovative features does Lennar include in its homes? As part of Everything's Included® our homes include innovative features that help simplify your everyday life. To learn about our home automation products, please visit the Smart home technology section of our Everything's® Included page . What is a Next Gen® home? Lennar's Next Gen - The Home Within a Home® offers innovative floorplans with a separate Next Gen® suite that functions as its own home, to accommodate your changing lifestyle without sacrificing comfort or cost savings, whether you need space for wellness, work, learning or family. To learn more about Lennar's Next Gen® homes, please visit our Next Gen page . What is the Opendoor and Trade up program about? <span> Lennar has partnered with Opendoor to create the New Home Trade-up Program. Sell your eligible home to Opendoor for a cash offer at a time that coordinates with the closing date of your new Lennar home to avoid double-moves, double-mortgages, and storage units. For more information, please visit our Lennar and Opendoor program page . How do I find the price for a new home? New home prices vary between Lennar and our subsidiary members (i.e., Village Builders, CalAtlantic, and WCI) and communities. Prices fluctuate based on design upgrades, homesite premiums, special incentives, and additional factors. To inquire about home prices in your community of interest,visit that community's page and contact a Consultant. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for your area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. Where can I register a client? If your client has a community of interest, contact the consultant for that community to register your client and verify that community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Do you have a rental properties available? Lennar has rental properties available with our subsidiary member, Quarterra, in some of the nation’s most popular cities. To learn more, visit the Quarterra website . How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? On this page, click Ask a question to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. How do I determine the taxes for a new home? You can contact a Consultant to determine the taxes for a new home however you should also consult your personal tax advisor. Are there Lennar vacation homes? We have a variety of dream homes in the most desired vacation cities. If you know what area you're interested in vacationing in, use the search tool at the top right corner of this page to begin your home search. If you need help, visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. What is an Active Adult Community? These Lennar communities offer residents ages 55 and better the ultimate in carefree, innovative living. Enjoy resort-style amenities and activities, as well as the chance to explore and make lifelong friends in some of the nation’s most popular cities to retire. Pursuant to the Fair Housing Act, these communities are subject to certain restrictions on the ages of occupants, and some buyers may not be eligible. To learn more, visit our Active Adult Communities page . Where does Lennar build new homes? Lennar currently builds in multiple states and popular real estate markets across the nation. To view a full list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page.

Homeowner What should I do if no one has contacted me about my existing warranty request? Please call your local Customer Care Team to inquire about your existing request and have the warranty request number available if you were sent one via email.
If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from live chat, message us, or call us to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I get the floorplan of my home? To retrieve the floorplan for your home, sign into your myLennar account and select "My home details" from the navigation bar. Scroll down to find your floorplan.* If your floorplan is unavailable, please contact your local Customer Care Team for assistance; you can email them from your myLennar dashboard by clicking on the Contact Customer Care link and submitting the form. *Please note: The floorplan that is available is the marketing floorplan for your homesite and not the detailed blueprint of construction drawings. Detailed blueprints of construction are a matter of company privacy.

Options and Upgrades Can I make changes to Lennar floorplans? Available changes to Lennar floorplans vary by community. Find your community of interest and contact a Consultant to learn more. Does Lennar have any green or energy saving initiatives or technologies? Other How can I apply for a career with Lennar? You can search and apply for available career opportunities with Lennar by visiting our Careers page . Please note that for each application, you will need to submit a copy of your resume. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for you area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. If I am a broker/agent, where do I find information for my client? If you know what community your client is interested in, visit that community's page and select ‘Contact a consultant’ to register your client and verify the community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Otherwise, select the drop down at the top of this page, click 'Contact us' and select 'Business inquiries.' Complete the Business inquiry form to have a consultant contact you regarding your client’s new home needs. How do I update my contact preferences? Sign in to your myLennar account to update your contact preferences. Once you sign in, click My account and then click Contacts to update your communication preferences. How can I suggest improvements for this site? To report errors with our website, select the drop down at the top of this page, click Contact us and select Feedback. Complete the Feedback form to tell us how we're doing or to share your thoughts.

Rancho Mission Viejo Schedule a tour Request info Rancho Mission Viejo Actively selling • $1 Millions Rancho Mission Viejo is an expansive master-planned community offering brand-new homes with an amenity-rich lifestyle in South Orange County, CA. Plan your visit 610 Furrow Rd, Rancho Mission Viejo, CA 92694 Get directions Open today from 10:00 to 5:00 See more hours Video Map + 25 photos Prices and features may vary and are subject to change. Photos are for illustrative purposes only. View legal disclaimer Exclusive amenities at Rancho Mission Viejo Rancho Mission Viejo is an extensive masterplan with a variety of onsite amenities designed for any lifestyle, with top-tier recreation right at your doorstep. No matter your passion, you’ll find it here. See all amenities Resort-inspired living Perfect for your unique lifestyle, the masterplan features a variety of swimming pools, including a 20,000-sq. ft. lagoon-style swimming pool within Ranch Cove, and another within Rienda with a spa, event room, BBQ area and tot lot. A wellness-focused lifestyle Effortlessly maintain an active lifestyle with a modern fitness center within Rienda, complete with a four-lane lap pool and fully equipped fitness room overlooking a luxurious pool and scenic hills. Top-tier recreation in your backyard At Rancho Mission Viejo, there’s always something to do right outside your doorstep. Located within the community, the Boulder Pond and Playground features a half-mile of walking paths surrounding a 3.5-acre pond with docks and a children’s playground. This map is for illustrative purposes and is subject to change. pages.text:
Move-in ready $1,019,990 2 bd  •  2 ba  •  2 Bay garage   •  1,451 ft² Nova 1 at 1203 Cottontail Rd., Rancho Mission Viejo, CA Actual configuration and features may vary. This new single-story home boasts a convenient and low-maintenance design. A bedroom and full bathroom are located off the foyer, near an inviting open-concept shared between the kitchen, living and dining areas with easy access to a covered patio. A lavish owner’s suite provides a private retreat at the back of the home, complete with a full bathroom and walk-in closet. Prices, dimensions and features may vary and are subject to change. Photos are for illustrative purposes only. Legal disclaimers Community brochure Homesite 0036 Plan Nova 1 Community Rancho Mission Viejo See property details Hear from our customers Floorplan Floorplan may vary by homesite. Learn more Everything’s included with this home This home comes with some of the most desired features included at no extra cost. Please see your New Home Consultant and/or home purchase agreement for actual features to be included in the home. Learn more See all features Kitchen Sleek quartz countertops throughout the kitchen Stainless steel gas cooktop (per plan) Stainless steel gas range with professional vent hood (per plan) + 16 more Interior ThermaTru® insulated fiberglass 8’ entry door Elegant paneled interior doors with smooth finish 4 ½" baseboards + 4 more Exterior Designer-coordinated exterior color and roof combinations Illuminated address lights Seagull® decorator-selected coach lights + 1 more Connectivity Legrand® On-Q® RF Transparent Structured Media® Enclosure Schlage Encode™ Smart WiFi Deadbolt Honeywell Home T6 Pro Wifi smart thermostat + 1 more What are the benefits of owning vs. renting? Owning a home can help you build equity, offers tax deductions and fixed monthly payments, and gives you a sense of permanence. When you rent, your lease sets the price, your place isn’t really yours, and there are no tax benefits or financial freedom. There are many benefits to owning a home that you can’t get with renting. Read More Was this helpful? Nova | Active Adult at Rancho Mission Viejo 610 Furrow Rd, Rancho Mission Viejo, CA 92694 Open today from 10:00 to 5:00 Nova at Gavilán Ridge is a collection of new active-adult homes in the Rancho Mission Viejo masterplan in Orange County, CA. Surrounded by 23,000 acres of the great outdoors, Rancho Mission Viejo offers residents resort-style amenities throughout the masterplan, including clubhouses, parks, pools, fitness centers, hiking trails and exclusive amenities for Gavilán residents. The prime location also puts homeowners close to everything south Orange County has to offer, including premium shopping, coastal beaches, botanical gardens and more. What are the benefits of owning vs. renting? Owning a home can help you build equity, offers tax deductions and fixed monthly payments, and gives you a sense of permanence. When you rent, your lease sets the price, your place isn’t really yours, and there are no tax benefits or financial freedom. There are many benefits to owning a home that you can’t get with renting. Move-in ready $1,085,990 2 bd  •  2 ba  •  2 Bay garage   •  1,451 ft² Nova 1 at 219 Bounty Rd., Rancho Mission Viejo, CA Actual configuration and features may vary. This new single-story home boasts a convenient and low-maintenance design. A bedroom and full bathroom are located off the foyer, near an inviting open-concept shared between the kitchen, living and dining areas with easy access to a covered patio. A lavish owner’s suite provides a private retreat at the back of the home, complete with a full bathroom and walk-in closet. Prices, dimensions and features may vary and are subject to change. Photos are for illustrative purposes only. Schedule a tour Request info This new two-story home is ready for the way families live today. A bedroom and full bathroom are located near the front of the home off the foyer, next to an inviting open-concept layout shared between the kitchen, living and dining areas with easy access to a covered patio. A lavish owner’s suite is nestled into a private rear corner, complete with a full bathroom and walk-in closet. A versatile bonus room makes up the second level, perfect for entertaining or enjoying personal hobbies. Schedule a tour Request info This new two-story home is ready for modern lifestyles. A bedroom with an en-suite bathroom is located near the front of the home off the foyer, next to a convenient den, ready to be used as an office or studio. A flexible open-concept floorplan boasts easy access to a covered patio for seamless indoor-outdoor living, while a luxurious owner’s suite offers a private retreat at the back of the home, complete with a full bathroom and walk-in closet. A versatile bonus room comprises the second floor, perfect for entertaining or personal hobbies. Frequently asked questions Community Community What is a Community Development District (CDD) ? The CDD refers to a community that assesses fees to pay for infrastructure and amenities within the community. Are there currently any promotions or incentives for this community? Contact a consultant for this community to inquire about any current promotions or incentives. Where can I see the floorplan of a home? Select a Plan from a Community page and scroll down to find the selection "View floorplan." How do I find out the grand opening date for this community? To receive information about this community's grand opening, contact a Consultant to have your name added to the interest list. How do I find out if a homesite is available? Please visit the community page for your community of interest to see a list of all available homesites. How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? Visit our Contact Us page and find the "I am looking for a home" label. Click Contact a consultant, select the location you are interested in and select fromthe live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about Where can I find a list of available homesites? Visit the community page for the community of your interest to see a list of all available homesites. Is this community pet friendly? To inquire about pet regulations in this community contact a Consultant. You can also ask about pet policies while visiting one of our Welcome Home Centers and speaking with a consultant, or you can call the Welcome Home Center. How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? Visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. Financing Who do I contact for a closing date on our home? Please contact your Consultant to inquire about the closing date on your new Lennar home. How can I obtain a copy of my appraisal? You should have received a copy of your appraisal prior to closing. Should it be lost, you can call either your Loan Officer or Loan Processor to have a new copy sent to you. How do I learn more about financing a home? Visit our Products and services page and click on the "Apply now" link to speak with our knowledgeable loan officers to learn more about financing your new home. Does Lennar offer financing options? Yes, Lennar Mortgage, LLC is our full-service mortgage lender and financial services subsidiary. Whether you are buying your first or fifth home, refinancing a current mortgage, or funding a home repair project, Lennar Mortgage provides the most appropriate loan options to suit your needs. To learn more, visit our Products and services page and then click the "Apply now" link. Who do I call regarding my loan status? Please reach out to your Loan Officer to inquire about your loan status. Once your loan has gone to an underwriter, the Loan Officer will notify you of this action and give you the name of the Loan Processor who will handle your file after the underwriter. The Loan Processor will contact you via a phone call to discuss your credit approval and next steps for closing on your new home. If the loan has already closed, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service. You can contact them by visiting https://www.lennarmortgage.com/Contact-Us . Where can I find my loan coupons? You should have received three loan coupons for the first three payments when you signed your closing documents. You should also receive monthly coupons in the mail the month payment is due.* If you require assistance, please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262. *Please note: The payment coupons are informational only and have no monetary value. A check or money order must be enclosed with the coupon to be processed. Who do I call if I want to lock-in my interest rate? Please contact your Loan Officer to handle the lock-in of an interest rate on your mortgage. Who do I make my mortgage payment out to when writing a check? You will make your payment out to "Lennar Mortgage, LLC" until your loan is sold. Once your loan is sold, you will receive a notification of its sale and contact information for the new servicer, to whom you will make out all future payments. Who do I contact to make a payment by telephone? Please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262 any time between 9am and 5pm (EST) Monday through Friday. Can I make my mortgage payments online? Please send your mortgage payments to Lennar Mortgage’s Loan Servicing Department at the following address:
Lennar Mortgage LLC Attn: Customer Service 140 Fountain Pkwy. N., Suite 250 Saint Petersburg, FL 33716 If you have any questions, call us toll free at 1-800-741-8262.
Please note: Once your loan is sold, you will receive a notification of its sale and contact information for the new servicer, to whom you will make all future payments. When is my first mortgage payment due? Your first mortgage payment is typically due 30 to 45 days after closing. To verify, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service at 1-800-741-8262 or via email at [email protected] . When are my mortgage payments due (each month)? Your mortgage payment is due on the 1st of the month, and after the 15th there is a late charge. If you fail to make your mortgage payment within 30 days, a late payment will be reported to the credit agencies. You should have received payment coupons at your closing, but you will also receive a payment notification in the mail shortly before payment is due. If you have any questions, please email [email protected] , or pay by phone by calling the toll-free number 1-877-648-7462. Who can I refer my friends and relatives to if they interested in applying for a mortgage? If you know someone who is interested in applying for a mortgage, please direct them to call your Loan Officer. Who do I contact to refinance my home? Please contact your Loan Officer to inquire about refinancing, or visit our Products and services page and click on the "Apply now" link  to find a Loan Officer near you. Who do I send my documentation to? The fastest, easiest way to send your documentation to Lennar Mortgage is through their Digital Mortgage app. The app is free and keeps private information secure with bank-level encryption. You also have the option to mail, fax, or use our secure web portal to send in your documents, but the app will provide you ease and flexibility to close your loan faster. Homebuying Where can I determine the actual cost of a home? Please contact a Consultant for your community of interest to get the accurate cost of your home of interest. Where do I find the base price for a home? Base prices for new Lennar homes are available on our community pages. Contact a Consultant on the page for your community of interest to inquire about home prices. Can I build a Lennar home on my own property? Lennar does not offer build-to-contract homes on personal property. Should your needs change, or you consider purchasing a new Lennar home in one of our communities, you can visit our Contact us page. Once there, select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. Do I need a broker/agent to purchase a new home? No, you do not need a broker or agent to purchase a new home from Lennar or one of our subsidiaries. While consultants represent Lennar and not customers, they are knowledgeable experts who are with you every step of your new home journey, answering questions and ensuring your smooth transition into homeownership. Of course, you are welcome to work with an independent broker or agent if you choose. Not all communities offer a broker co-op commission. If I have a broker/agent, do they need to visit the community with me? An agent or broker is required to accompany and register with you on your first visit to the Welcome Home Center at your community of interest in order to qualify for a broker co-op commission. Select communities and areas may not offer a broker co-op commission, regardless of timing and registration, but you are always welcome to be accompanied by a broker or agent of your choice. Does Lennar have any future communities planned for my area? Lennar's building operations expand to include new markets every year. To see the most up-to-date list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page. What are the Homeowners Association (HOA) fees for my community of interest? You can find an approximate HOA fee on the community page on the website or you can contact a Consultant for your community of interest to learn about the HOA fees. What kind of home tours does Lennar offer? Lennar offers three ways to tour, a Virtual tour, a Self-Guided tour, and a Lennar Guided tour. The virtual tour is a combination of high-quality, 3D rendering, or a panoramic view of a model home. The self-guided tour is an onsite tour of a model home at your own pace. The Lennar Guided tour is an in-person one-on-one tour of a model home with a Consultant. Does Lennar offer an incentives for first time homebuyers or current Lennar homeowners? Promotions and incentives vary by community. Contact a home consultant for your community of interest to inquire about current incentives.
Screen reader support enabled. What is the meaning of homesite premium? Lennar offers premium options for those who want a larger home or one that is situated in a more desirable area of the community. For example, if you wish to live in a Lennar community with a golf course and own a home on the course, you might pay more for that desirable homesite. The same applies for the purchase of a larger homesite (property). Contact a Consultant for your community of interest to inquire about homesite premiums. What kind of innovative features does Lennar include in its homes? As part of Everything's Included® our homes include innovative features that help simplify your everyday life. To learn about our home automation products, please visit the Smart home technology section of our Everything's® Included page . What is a Next Gen® home? Lennar's Next Gen - The Home Within a Home® offers innovative floorplans with a separate Next Gen® suite that functions as its own home, to accommodate your changing lifestyle without sacrificing comfort or cost savings, whether you need space for wellness, work, learning or family. To learn more about Lennar's Next Gen® homes, please visit our Next Gen page . What is the Opendoor and Trade up program about? <span> Lennar has partnered with Opendoor to create the New Home Trade-up Program. Sell your eligible home to Opendoor for a cash offer at a time that coordinates with the closing date of your new Lennar home to avoid double-moves, double-mortgages, and storage units. For more information, please visit our Lennar and Opendoor program page . How do I find the price for a new home? New home prices vary between Lennar and our subsidiary members (i.e., Village Builders, CalAtlantic, and WCI) and communities. Prices fluctuate based on design upgrades, homesite premiums, special incentives, and additional factors. To inquire about home prices in your community of interest,visit that community's page and contact a Consultant. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for your area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. Where can I register a client? If your client has a community of interest, contact the consultant for that community to register your client and verify that community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Do you have a rental properties available? Lennar has rental properties available with our subsidiary member, Quarterra, in some of the nation’s most popular cities. To learn more, visit the Quarterra website . How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? On this page, click Ask a question to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. How do I determine the taxes for a new home? You can contact a Consultant to determine the taxes for a new home however you should also consult your personal tax advisor. Are there Lennar vacation homes? We have a variety of dream homes in the most desired vacation cities. If you know what area you're interested in vacationing in, use the search tool at the top right corner of this page to begin your home search. If you need help, visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. What is an Active Adult Community? These Lennar communities offer residents ages 55 and better the ultimate in carefree, innovative living. Enjoy resort-style amenities and activities, as well as the chance to explore and make lifelong friends in some of the nation’s most popular cities to retire. Pursuant to the Fair Housing Act, these communities are subject to certain restrictions on the ages of occupants, and some buyers may not be eligible. To learn more, visit our Active Adult Communities page . Where does Lennar build new homes? Lennar currently builds in multiple states and popular real estate markets across the nation. To view a full list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page. Homeowner What should I do if no one has contacted me about my existing warranty request? Please call your local Customer Care Team to inquire about your existing request and have the warranty request number available if you were sent one via email.
If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from live chat, message us, or call us to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Who do I contact about obtaining my mortgage interest statement for income tax purposes? Please contact your mortgage lender to inquire about your mortgage interest statement and how you can obtain a copy for income tax purposes. What is the phone number for my community's HOA? You can reach out to your local Customer Care Team to help you retrieve your HOA's phone number. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I find information regarding my previously owned/current Lennar home (paint colors, cabinet manufacturer, etc.)? To receive more information on your home, please contact your local Customer Care Team. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Who do I contact if I have questions or concerns about a warranty? To inquire about a warranty, please contact the local Customer Care team for your area. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. What are the Home Owner's Association (HOA) fees for my community? You can reach out to your local Customer Care Team to help you retrieve this information. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I get the floorplan of my home? To retrieve the floorplan for your home, sign into your myLennar account and select "My home details" from the navigation bar. Scroll down to find your floorplan.* If your floorplan is unavailable, please contact your local Customer Care Team for assistance; you can email them from your myLennar dashboard by clicking on the Contact Customer Care link and submitting the form. *Please note: The floorplan that is available is the marketing floorplan for your homesite and not the detailed blueprint of construction drawings. Detailed blueprints of construction are a matter of company privacy. Options and Upgrades Can I make changes to Lennar floorplans? Available changes to Lennar floorplans vary by community. Find your community of interest and contact a Consultant to learn more. What kind of interior design and add-on options are available for my home? To learn more about interior design and add-on options, contact a consultant for your community of interest. Can I customize my home or get additional upgrades? To learn more about customizations and additional upgrades, contact a consultant for your community of interest. Does Lennar have any green or energy saving initiatives or technologies? Yes, Lennar homes are built with energy-efficient construction and features which vary by community but can include solar panels, LED lighting, and ENERGY STAR® certified appliances. To learn more about available energy-saving features, visit our Everything’s Included® page and click on the Energy-conscious features link or you can view the list of “Included Features” on a Community page. Other How can I apply for a career with Lennar? You can search and apply for available career opportunities with Lennar by visiting our Careers page . Please note that for each application, you will need to submit a copy of your resume. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for you area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. If I am a broker/agent, where do I find information for my client? If you know what community your client is interested in, visit that community's page and select ‘Contact a consultant’ to register your client and verify the community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Otherwise, select the drop down at the top of this page, click 'Contact us' and select 'Business inquiries.' Complete the Business inquiry form to have a consultant contact you regarding your client’s new home needs. How do I update my contact preferences? Sign in to your myLennar account to update your contact preferences. Once you sign in, click My account and then click Contacts to update your communication preferences. How can I suggest improvements for this site? To report errors with our website, select the drop down at the top of this page, click Contact us and select Feedback. Complete the Feedback form to tell us how we're doing or to share your thoughts.
When completing the form, be sure to select "Website errors" under Select a topic. If you want to suggest improvements, select "Comments " from the topic options .

If you have any questions, please email [email protected] , or pay by phone by calling the toll-free number 1-877-648-7462. Who can I refer my friends and relatives to if they interested in applying for a mortgage? If you know someone who is interested in applying for a mortgage, please direct them to call your Loan Officer. Who do I contact to refinance my home? Please contact your Loan Officer to inquire about refinancing, or visit our Products and services page and click on the "Apply now" link  to find a Loan Officer near you. Who do I send my documentation to? The fastest, easiest way to send your documentation to Lennar Mortgage is through their Digital Mortgage app. The app is free and keeps private information secure with bank-level encryption. You also have the option to mail, fax, or use our secure web portal to send in your documents, but the app will provide you ease and flexibility to close your loan faster.

Who do I contact for a closing date on our home? Please contact your Consultant to inquire about the closing date on your new Lennar home. How can I obtain a copy of my appraisal? How do I learn more about financing a home? Yes, Lennar Mortgage, LLC is our full-service mortgage lender and financial services subsidiary. To learn more, visit our Products and services page and then click the "Apply now" link. Who do I call regarding my loan status? Please reach out to your Loan Officer to inquire about your loan status. Once your loan has gone to an underwriter, the Loan Officer will notify you of this action and give you the name of the Loan Processor who will handle your file after the underwriter. The Loan Processor will contact you via a phone call to discuss your credit approval and next steps for closing on your new home. If the loan has already closed, please contact Lennar Mortgage – Customer Service. You can contact them by visiting https://www.lennarmortgage.com/Contact-Us . Where can I find my loan coupons? You should have received three loan coupons for the first three payments when you signed your closing documents. You should also receive monthly coupons in the mail the month payment is due.* If you require assistance, please contact the Lennar Mortgage Loan Servicing Department at the toll-free number 1-800-741-8262. *Please note: The payment coupons are informational only and have no monetary value. A check or money order must be enclosed with the coupon to be processed. Who do I call if I want to lock-in my interest rate? Please contact your Loan Officer to handle the lock-in of an interest rate on your mortgage. Who do I make my mortgage payment out to when writing a check? You will make your payment out to "Lennar Mortgage, LLC" until your loan is sold. Once your loan is sold, you will receive a notification of its sale and contact information for the new servicer, to whom you will make out all future payments. Homebuying Where can I determine the actual cost of a home? Please contact a Consultant for your community of interest to get the accurate cost of your home of interest. Where do I find the base price for a home? Base prices for new Lennar homes are available on our community pages. Contact a Consultant on the page for your community of interest to inquire about home prices. Can I build a Lennar home on my own property? Lennar does not offer build-to-contract homes on personal property. Should your needs change, or you consider purchasing a new Lennar home in one of our communities, you can visit our Contact us page. Once there, select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. Do I need a broker/agent to purchase a new home? No, you do not need a broker or agent to purchase a new home from Lennar or one of our subsidiaries. While consultants represent Lennar and not customers, they are knowledgeable experts who are with you every step of your new home journey, answering questions and ensuring your smooth transition into homeownership. Of course, you are welcome to work with an independent broker or agent if you choose. Not all communities offer a broker co-op commission. If I have a broker/agent, do they need to visit the community with me? An agent or broker is required to accompany and register with you on your first visit to the Welcome Home Center at your community of interest in order to qualify for a broker co-op commission. Select communities and areas may not offer a broker co-op commission, regardless of timing and registration, but you are always welcome to be accompanied by a broker or agent of your choice. Does Lennar have any future communities planned for my area? Lennar's building operations expand to include new markets every year. To see the most up-to-date list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page. What are the Homeowners Association (HOA) fees for my community of interest? You can find an approximate HOA fee on the community page on the website or you can contact a Consultant for your community of interest to learn about the HOA fees. What kind of home tours does Lennar offer? Lennar offers three ways to tour, a Virtual tour, a Self-Guided tour, and a Lennar Guided tour. The virtual tour is a combination of high-quality, 3D rendering, or a panoramic view of a model home. The self-guided tour is an onsite tour of a model home at your own pace. The Lennar Guided tour is an in-person one-on-one tour of a model home with a Consultant. Does Lennar offer an incentives for first time homebuyers or current Lennar homeowners? Promotions and incentives vary by community. Contact a home consultant for your community of interest to inquire about current incentives.
Screen reader support enabled. What is the meaning of homesite premium? Lennar offers premium options for those who want a larger home or one that is situated in a more desirable area of the community. For example, if you wish to live in a Lennar community with a golf course and own a home on the course, you might pay more for that desirable homesite. The same applies for the purchase of a larger homesite (property). Contact a Consultant for your community of interest to inquire about homesite premiums. What kind of innovative features does Lennar include in its homes? As part of Everything's Included® our homes include innovative features that help simplify your everyday life. To learn about our home automation products, please visit the Smart home technology section of our Everything's® Included page . What is a Next Gen® home? Lennar's Next Gen - The Home Within a Home® offers innovative floorplans with a separate Next Gen® suite that functions as its own home, to accommodate your changing lifestyle without sacrificing comfort or cost savings, whether you need space for wellness, work, learning or family. To learn more about Lennar's Next Gen® homes, please visit our Next Gen page . What is the Opendoor and Trade up program about? <span> Lennar has partnered with Opendoor to create the New Home Trade-up Program. Sell your eligible home to Opendoor for a cash offer at a time that coordinates with the closing date of your new Lennar home to avoid double-moves, double-mortgages, and storage units. For more information, please visit our Lennar and Opendoor program page . How do I find the price for a new home? New home prices vary between Lennar and our subsidiary members (i.e., Village Builders, CalAtlantic, and WCI) and communities. Prices fluctuate based on design upgrades, homesite premiums, special incentives, and additional factors. To inquire about home prices in your community of interest,visit that community's page and contact a Consultant. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for your area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. Where can I register a client? If your client has a community of interest, contact the consultant for that community to register your client and verify that community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Do you have a rental properties available? Lennar has rental properties available with our subsidiary member, Quarterra, in some of the nation’s most popular cities. To learn more, visit the Quarterra website . How do I speak to a Consultant in my area of interest? On this page, click Ask a question to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. How do I determine the taxes for a new home? You can contact a Consultant to determine the taxes for a new home however you should also consult your personal tax advisor. Are there Lennar vacation homes? We have a variety of dream homes in the most desired vacation cities. If you know what area you're interested in vacationing in, use the search tool at the top right corner of this page to begin your home search. If you need help, visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest. What is an Active Adult Community? These Lennar communities offer residents ages 55 and better the ultimate in carefree, innovative living. Enjoy resort-style amenities and activities, as well as the chance to explore and make lifelong friends in some of the nation’s most popular cities to retire. Pursuant to the Fair Housing Act, these communities are subject to certain restrictions on the ages of occupants, and some buyers may not be eligible. To learn more, visit our Active Adult Communities page . Where does Lennar build new homes? Lennar currently builds in multiple states and popular real estate markets across the nation. To view a full list of the cities and states where we build, scroll down to the bottom of this page.

If you know what area you're interested in vacationing in, use the search tool at the top right corner of this page to begin your home search. If you need help, visit our Contact Us page and select from the live chat, message, or call us options to speak with a consultant about your area of interest.

What is an Active Adult Community? These Lennar communities offer residents ages 55 and better the ultimate in carefree, innovative living. Enjoy resort-style amenities and activities, as well as the chance to explore and make lifelong friends in some of the nation’s most popular cities to retire. Pursuant to the Fair Housing Act, these communities are subject to certain restrictions on the ages of occupants, and some buyers may not be eligible. To learn more, visit our Active Adult Communities page .

Homeowner What should I do if no one has contacted me about my existing warranty request? Please call your local Customer Care Team to inquire about your existing request and have the warranty request number available if you were sent one via email.
If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from live chat, message us, or call us to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Who do I contact about obtaining my mortgage interest statement for income tax purposes? Please contact your mortgage lender to inquire about your mortgage interest statement and how you can obtain a copy for income tax purposes. What is the phone number for my community's HOA? You can reach out to your local Customer Care Team to help you retrieve your HOA's phone number. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I find information regarding my previously owned/current Lennar home (paint colors, cabinet manufacturer, etc.)? Who do I contact if I have questions or concerns about a warranty? To inquire about a warranty, please contact the local Customer Care team for your area. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. What are the Home Owner's Association (HOA) fees for my community? You can reach out to your local Customer Care Team to help you retrieve this information. If you do not have their contact information, visit our Contact us page and select from the live chat, message us, or call us options to talk to a consultant who will help you locate your customer care team contact. Where can I get the floorplan of my home? To retrieve the floorplan for your home, sign into your myLennar account and select "My home details" from the navigation bar. Scroll down to find your floorplan.* If your floorplan is unavailable, please contact your local Customer Care Team for assistance; you can email them from your myLennar dashboard by clicking on the Contact Customer Care link and submitting the form. *Please note: The floorplan that is available is the marketing floorplan for your homesite and not the detailed blueprint of construction drawings. Detailed blueprints of construction are a matter of company privacy.

Options and Upgrades Can I make changes to Lennar floorplans? Available changes to Lennar floorplans vary by community. Find your community of interest and contact a Consultant to learn more. What kind of interior design and add-on options are available for my home? To learn more about interior design and add-on options, contact a consultant for your community of interest. Can I customize my home or get additional upgrades? To learn more about customizations and additional upgrades, contact a consultant for your community of interest. Does Lennar have any green or energy saving initiatives or technologies? Yes, Lennar homes are built with energy-efficient construction and features which vary by community but can include solar panels, LED lighting, and ENERGY STAR® certified appliances. To learn more about available energy-saving features, visit our Everything’s Included® page and click on the Energy-conscious features link or you can view the list of “Included Features” on a Community page.

Other How can I apply for a career with Lennar? You can search and apply for available career opportunities with Lennar by visiting our Careers page . Please note that for each application, you will need to submit a copy of your resume. How much are you offering realtors in commission? Commission rates for real estate agents vary by location. To inquire about these rates, contact a Lennar Consultant for you area of interest. To qualify for any applicable commission/bonus, registration must occur on the first visit and a broker/agent must execute Lennar's Cooperating Broker Agreement and otherwise comply with Lennar's Broker Participation Policy. Offers valid only in states where permitted. If I am a broker/agent, where do I find information for my client? If you know what community your client is interested in, visit that community's page and select ‘Contact a consultant’ to register your client and verify the community’s broker co-op commission participation status. Otherwise, select the drop down at the top of this page, click 'Contact us' and select 'Business inquiries.' Complete the Business inquiry form to have a consultant contact you regarding your client’s new home needs. How do I update my contact preferences? Sign in to your myLennar account to update your contact preferences. Once you sign in, click My account and then click Contacts to update your communication preferences. How can I suggest improvements for this site? To report errors with our website, select the drop down at the top of this page, click Contact us and select Feedback. Complete the Feedback form to tell us how we're doing or to share your thoughts.
